series_context.home_court_note: Home team won all first six games in the series.
series_context.investment_context: No investment. Postmortem logged per playoff protocol.

model_projection_vs_outcome.available_projection_data: No actionable investment and no formal model projection was recorded for this matchup in the current chat window.
model_projection_vs_outcome.directional_read: Series/roster profile only. Toronto forced pace off turnovers and transition while Cleveland controlled the glass but lost the possession-value battle through turnovers.

Q1.note: High efficiency opening quarter; both teams scored 32. Toronto had 9 assists and only 1 turnover.

Q2.note: Toronto built separation with 5 made threes, 7 assists, and forced Cleveland into 31.8% shooting.

Q3.note: Toronto pushed lead to 15 behind Barnes orchestration and Murray-Boyles pressure, but Cleveland's second-chance volume kept them within range.

Q4.note: Toronto nearly collapsed: 4-17 FG, 1-7 from three, 5 turnovers. Cleveland tied the game at 104 with 11.6 seconds left.

OT1.note: RJ Barrett hit the winning 29-foot three with 1.2 seconds left; Mobley missed final three-point attempt.

first_half.note: Scottie Barnes had 14 points and 10 assists by halftime; Toronto led by 10.

regulation.note: Cleveland erased an 11-point fourth-quarter deficit to force overtime.

game_flow_analysis.primary_swing: Toronto won the transition/turnover battle while Cleveland won the glass. Toronto generated 25 points off Cleveland's 18 turnovers and a 20-6 fastbreak edge.
game_flow_analysis.cleveland_recovery: Cleveland's fourth-quarter defense and offensive rebounding erased an 11-point deficit; Toronto scored only 12 in Q4 on 23.5% shooting.
game_flow_analysis.final_sequence: In overtime, Cleveland led 110-109 with under 11 seconds left after Toronto intentionally fouled. Evan Mobley lost the ball out of bounds at 10.9 seconds. RJ Barrett then hit a 29-foot three with 1.2 seconds left. Mobley's final three missed.
game_flow_analysis.deciding_edge: Toronto's extra pressure events and perimeter shotmaking outweighed Cleveland's +14 total rebound edge and +12 second-chance point edge.

rebound_environment_analysis.cleveland_edge: +14 total rebounds, +9 offensive rebounds, 22 second-chance points
rebound_environment_analysis.toronto_profile: Toronto did not win the glass, but created enough transition and turnover pressure to neutralize Cleveland's twin-tower rebounding edge.
funnel_notes: Mobley 14 rebounds with 4 offensive rebounds confirms primary Cleveland glass funnel.
Harden added 5 offensive rebounds from guard/long-miss positioning, a hidden possession extender.
Barrett's 9 rebounds and Murray-Boyles' 4 offensive rebounds were Toronto's key counterweights.
Poeltl played only 21:31, so Toronto's rebound survival relied on Barnes/Barrett/Murray-Boyles rather than traditional center anchoring.

TOR: Brandon Ingram was inactive with right heel inflammation.
Immanuel Quickley was inactive with right hamstring strain.
Barnes played 47:57 and functioned as the primary initiator.
Walter and Barrett both logged 43+ minutes, confirming shortened playoff wing rotation.
CLE: Harden played 44:00 and handled major creation responsibility.
Mobley played 41:12 and was the main two-way frontcourt engine.
Mitchell played 40:58 with high shot burden but inefficient from three.

modify.reason: Cleveland generated rim/paint production and rebounding, but 18 turnovers and 26.8% from three suppressed total possession value.
Toronto nearly gave away an 11-point fourth-quarter lead with a 12-point Q4 and 5 turnovers; add late-game pressure penalty when primary handler load exceeds 45 minutes.
Barrett had 5 turnovers and was scoreless in Q4 but hit the OT winner; retain high-variance shotmaking but widen distribution tails.

retraining_flags.description: Cleveland won rebounds 52-38 and second-chance points 22-10, but lost due to 18 turnovers leading to 25 Toronto points and a 20-6 fastbreak deficit.
Barnes produced 25 points and 14 assists in 47:57 with Ingram and Quickley out. Maintain high usage and assist projection when Toronto is short on creators.
Toronto scored only 12 points in Q4 on 4-17 shooting and 5 turnovers. Apply late-game fragility when lead-protection mode appears and shot creation narrows.
Cleveland collected 19 offensive rebounds and 22 second-chance points. Mobley/Allen/Harden offensive board roles deserve elevated Game 7 rebounding inputs.
